record.field: Cal.com is the leading open-source scheduling platform and a self-hostable alternative to Calendly that puts you in full control of your data, branding, and workflows.

Cal.com is the leading open-source scheduling platform and a self-hostable alternative to Calendly that puts you in full control of your data, branding, and workflows.

It lets individuals and teams share booking links so others can schedule meetings without back-and-forth emails. Built on a modern TypeScript stack (Next.js, tRPC, Prisma, PostgreSQL), it offers calendar sync, video conferencing, payments, and an API-first, white-label design. Self-hosting is a first-class use case, giving organizations data residency and the ability to deploy on their own domain.

Key features
  • Personal and team booking pages with customizable event types and availability rules
  • Two-way calendar sync with Google, Microsoft/Outlook and CalDAV providers
  • Built-in video conferencing integrations (Zoom, Google Meet, Daily.co) and payment collection
  • 100+ integrations plus a documented API and webhooks for automation
  • White-label theming, custom domains and embeddable booking widgets
  • Routing forms, round-robin and collective scheduling for teams
Best for

Privacy-conscious teams, agencies and organizations that want a self-hosted, data-sovereign scheduling layer, and developers who need a programmable booking engine to embed into their own products.

services: postgres: image: postgres:16-alpine volumes: - ./calcom_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: - POSTGRES_USER=postgres - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password - POSTGRES_DB=db - DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:password@postgres:5432/db container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal calcom: image: calcom/cal.com:v2.7.6 depends_on: - postgres environment: - NEXTAUTH_SECRET=${NEXTAUTH_SECRET} - CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY=${CALENDSO_ENCRYPTION_KEY} - DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:password@postgres:5432/db - NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBAPP_URL=http://{{ instance.full_domain }} - NEXTAUTH_URL=http://{{ instance.full_domain }}/api/auth container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
